name: PR3 Porto Furado Trail
name_original: Percurso circular do Porto Furado (PR 3) — Parque Natural de Montesinho
description: Hike the moderate 5.0-mile PR3 Porto Furado Trail in Montesinho Natural Park, featuring granite formations and reservoir views.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around França traverse a landscape characterized by natural parks, dense forests, and river valleys. The region offers varied terrain, from ancient woodlands to paths alongside waterways. These routes provide opportunities to explore the natural environment, often featuring gentle gradients suitable for all skill levels. Best easy hiking trails around França The most popular easy hiking route is Bosque de Tejedelo — circular by Requejo de Sanabria and natural park Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores, a 3.0 miles (4.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete, winding through an ancient forest. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11) — circular route in the Montesinho Natural Park, an easy 4.3 miles (6.8 km) path. This route explores the natural park and follows a river. Local hikers also love the Montesinho Natural Park – Montesinho village loop from Montezinho, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through Montesinho Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Hiking around França is defined by natural parks, forested areas, and river landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, focusing on accessible and enjoyable paths. The routes in França are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore França's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around França?
A: França offers a selection of easy hiking trails, with at least 4 routes specifically categorized as easy. In total, the region features 15 hiking routes, providing various options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy circular walks in França?
A: Yes, França has several easy circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19880104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baceiro River Ford loop from Vilarinho&lt;/a&gt; is an easy circular hike. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17540813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Domus Municipalis de Bragança – Bragança Castle loop from Bragança&lt;/a&gt;, which is also an easy circular trail.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on easy hikes around França?
A: Easy hikes around França typically traverse natural parks, dense forests, and river valleys. You'll encounter varied terrain, from ancient woodlands to paths alongside waterways, often with gentle gradients. Expect scenic views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4945816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Montesinho&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4954373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesinho Hills&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly easy walks in França?
A: Many of the easy trails in França are suitable for families due to their gentle gradients and manageable distances. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022985701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Tejedelo — circular by Requejo de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through an ancient forest, are popular choices for families looking for an enjoyable outdoor experience.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see near easy hiking trails?
A: Along easy hiking trails, you can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17540813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Domus Municipalis de Bragança – Bragança Castle loop from Bragança&lt;/a&gt; takes you past historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4473005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bragança Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4715353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Domus Municipalis de Bragança&lt;/a&gt;. Other trails might lead you to natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3420090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejedelo Forest&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Weir and Boardwalk of the Rio Onor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the easy trails in França?
A: The easy trails in França are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's natural parks, the tranquility of the forested areas, and the scenic river landscapes, highlighting the accessible and enjoyable paths.

Q: Are there any easy hikes that follow a river or have water features?
A: Yes, several easy hikes in França feature water elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2172563424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11) — circular route in the Montesinho Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; follows a river. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Weir and Boardwalk of the Rio Onor&lt;/a&gt; along some routes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Serrada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another notable water feature in the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in França?
A: França's climate generally allows for pleasant hiking during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons offer mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery, making them ideal for exploring the region's easy trails. Winter can also be suitable, though some paths might be affected by weather conditions.

Q: Are there easy hikes in natural parks around França?
A: Absolutely. França is characterized by its natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2172563424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11) — circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesinho Natural Park – Montesinho village loop&lt;/a&gt; both take you through the beautiful Montesinho Natural Park, offering accessible paths within these protected areas.

Q: What should I wear for an easy hike in França?
A: For easy hikes in França, comfortable walking shoes or light hiking boots are recommended. Dress in layers to adapt to changing weather, and consider bringing a light rain jacket, especially during spring and autumn. Sun protection is advisable during warmer months.

Q: Are there any easy hikes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, many easy trails in França provide opportunities for scenic views. The region's natural parks and hilly terrain often feature elevated spots. Look for routes that pass through areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4945816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Montesinho&lt;/a&gt;, where you can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4954373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Montesinho Hills&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
The Montesinho Natural Park covers an area of approximately 75 thousand hectares and is located in the Terra Fria Transmontana region, in the extreme northeast of Portugal, bordering the Spanish communities of Galicia and Castile-Leon. It covers the territories of two municipalities, namely Bragança and Vinhais.

As a curiosity, you should know that there are more than 90 villages within the Montesinho Natural Park and that the highest point in the park is the Serra do Montesinho, whose peak is 1,486 metres above sea level.

It covers the territories of two municipalities, namely Bragança and Vinhais.

As a curiosity, you should know that there are more than 90 villages within the Montesinho Natural Park and that the highest point in the park is the Serra do Montesinho, whose peak is 1,486 metres above sea level.
It has a dreamlike landscape here, very quiet and ideal for relaxation

The Serra Serrada Dam, built on the bed of the Andorinhas river, is located in the parish of França, in the municipality of Bragança, Bragança District, in Portugal. The dam was designed in 1986 and began operating in 1989.

faqs.question: Where does the PR3 Porto Furado Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The PR3 Porto Furado Trail is a circular route that typically starts and ends in Montesinho Village. You can usually find parking available within the village itself, which serves as a convenient base for accessing the trailhead.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is characterized by its granite geology, featuring large slabs, boulders, and loose blocks. You'll also encounter small watercourses. Be aware that some sections can be overgrown with brambles and dense vegetation, so wearing long trousers is recommended.

faqs.question: What are the main natural and historical highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience the scenic landscape of Parque Natural de Montesinho. Notable features include the Serra Serrada Reservoir, impressive rounded granite formations, and glimpses of ancient occupation through Roman dam and canal remains, as well as the Castro Curisco rock art complex.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the PR3 Porto Furado Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse flora throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. During summer, some sections might be overgrown, and temperatures can be higher, while winter can bring colder weather and potentially snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: While often elusive, the area is home to animals like foxes, wild boars, and roe deer. If you're very lucky, you might even spot wolves. Smaller mammals such as rabbits and hares are more commonly seen.

faqs.question: Is the PR3 Porto Furado Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's located within Montesinho Natural Park, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Be mindful of any specific park regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for hiking in Montesinho Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for hiking the PR3 Porto Furado Trail within Montesinho Natural Park. However, always respect park rules, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and carrying out all your trash.

faqs.question: Is the PR3 Porto Furado Trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate difficulty, the trail might be challenging for very young children or those unaccustomed to hiking. Older children with some hiking experience should manage well, especially if they are prepared for the varied terrain and potential overgrown sections. Consider the 3-hour estimated duration when planning with family.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for overgrown sections, we recommend wearing long trousers to protect against brambles. Sturdy hiking boots are essential for navigating the granite outcrops and uneven paths. Bring sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and a map or GPS device like komoot, as mobile signal can be intermittent.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The trail offers broad views to the south, limited by the Serra da Nogueira. From the highest points, you can enjoy extensive panoramic views, particularly over the Sabor River valley, and a specific View of the Montesinho Hills.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Montesinho Village, where the trail typically starts, is a small settlement. While it might not have extensive facilities, you may find a local cafe or guesthouse offering refreshments. It's advisable to bring your own supplies and use facilities before starting your hike.

waypoints.name: Scenic landscape in Parque Natural de Montesinho
